Who are we protecting?
This shapes every reply Guardian gives you from this moment on.
Where are you in the process?
Tell Guardian about your case and every answer, every reply, and every statement becomes specific to you and your child.
Select all that apply
Let's figure out where you are
📬 Received papers in the post? — They have made an application. Select Active C100.
📋 Already been to court? — There is an active case. Select Active C100 or Back in court.
📄 Have a court order but they're ignoring it? — Select C79 and Order in place.
🚫 Have a non-molestation order? — Select Non-molestation order involved.
🆕 Nothing happened yet? — Select No court proceedings yet.
You can update this later in My Case as your situation changes.
Not sure? Upload your court order later and Guardian will find it.
Helps Guardian anchor your diary history. Skip if you don't know or it's disputed.
This shapes how Guardian words every reply — one wrong phrase can be misread as trying to re-engage.
Tell Guardian what's been happening
The more accurate the profile is, the better Guardian can keep claims, dates and sources organised. This stays on your device unless sync is enabled.
CalmVery difficult
5 / 10
Write it how you'd say it. One allegation per line. You can add more later.
Even small wins matter. Include everything that has gone in your favour.
Upload your case documents
Add useful documents now or skip and do it later. During testing, use fictional or non-sensitive material first.
YOUR DOCUMENTS
Upload anything you have — court orders, Cafcass letters, statements, messages. Guardian works out what each document is and files it automatically. Or skip this and add documents later from Case File.
MESSAGE HISTORY
Upload your message history with the other parent. Guardian reads it and builds a picture of the communication pattern.
📱
WhatsApp export
Tap here for step-by-step instructions
›
HOW TO EXPORT YOUR WHATSAPP CHAT
On Android:
1. Open WhatsApp → open the chat with the other parent
2. Tap the three dots (top right) → More → Export chat
3. Tap Without media
4. Share the .txt file to yourself (email, notes, files app)
5. Upload the .txt file below

On iPhone:
1. Open WhatsApp → open the chat
2. Tap the name at the top → Export Chat
3. Choose Without Media
4. Save to Files or share to yourself
5. Upload below
OR JUST TELL US YOUR CASE
Speak your case in your own words
What happened, how long, what you're dealing with. Guardian listens.
Who are you dealing with?
This helps Guardian choose a calmer tone and avoid replies that accidentally accept disputed claims.
HOW DO THEY COMMUNICATE?
ANYTHING ELSE GUARDIAN SHOULD KNOW ABOUT THEM? (optional)
Let Guardian find your voice
Paste 10–20 of your sent messages so Guardian learns how you write. Every reply will sound like you — not a robot.
Go to WhatsApp → your conversation → copy your last 10–20 sent messages → paste them below.
YOUR SENT MESSAGES
Don't worry about removing timestamps or names — Guardian filters those out.

WHEN YOU NEED TO GO FURTHER
How Guardian works
1. Understand what happened in Chat.
2. Save the useful facts into Diary.
3. Attach photos, messages, school or medical evidence.
4. Build statements from saved facts when needed.
5. Export a court bundle, personal record or evidence timeline.

Continuing previous analysis

Upload screenshots, PDFs, or documents — up to 20 files at once. Guardian reads everything: timestamps, sender names, every word.

📷 Android tip
Use your phone's scroll screenshot feature to capture the whole conversation in one long image. Samsung, Pixel, and most Android phones have this built in — look for “Scroll capture” after taking a screenshot.
🔒
Your screenshots are private. They are sent for AI reading during this check and are not saved to Evidence unless you choose to add them. During testing, use fictional or non-sensitive screenshots only.

Protective reply
Before I suggest a reply
The more accurate the details are, the safer and clearer the reply can be.
Do you need to send this?
Silence is sometimes the strongest reply
Checking in without a specific reason can open the door to conflict. Unless there is a health concern or something urgent — waiting until your next contact is often the stronger position.
Their message
⚠️ Do not send this yet
This reply could make things worse. Here is why:
💬 Chat
Take a breath — 15s before you send. Rushed replies can be used against you.
What is not right?
Choose a reason or explain it. Guardian will remember the preference.
✏️ Tap the reply above to edit before sending
💡 What to do now tap to expand
How does this feel?
Guardian builds from your answers — edit until it sounds like you.
Only relevant selected information affects this score. Untick anything unrelated or incorrect.
This correction is used for this reply only. It does not alter your permanent case history.
Now preserve the whole thread
After sending, add the whole message thread to your Diary: what came before, their message, your reply, and what came after. Guardian learns the sequence, not just one isolated message.
Was this helpful?
Thanks — that helps us improve Guardian.

Add Evidence
One place for every important record
Add the original file where possible. Guardian keeps the file, its source, the event date and its analysis separate.
1. Identify
Message, photo, voice note, video, court paper, nursery or AI notes.
2. Separate
Original evidence stays separate from Guardian analysis.
3. Link
Connect it to a diary entry if you have a D-reference.
4. Review
Check the analysis before relying on it.
If this supports a diary entry, use the D-reference shown on the diary card. Leave blank if unsure.
Add a Drive, Dropbox, YouTube or webpage link
A link records where material was found, including CCTV or video too large for Guardian's 100 MB video limit. It can change, expire or be deleted, so it is not treated as the evidence original. Keep an untouched offline copy.
Bulk case import and AI notes
Use this when you have several files at once. Guardian treats conversations from another AI as reference notes, not original evidence.
How to export WhatsApp: open the conversation, choose Export chat, select Without media, then upload the TXT file. Upload important screenshots, photos or voice-note transcripts separately.

ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini or another AI: export or save the conversation as PDF, TXT, DOCX or screenshots. Guardian keeps this as AI reference material only and asks you to verify all important points against the real source.
Audio and video are preserved as originals with a hash. Guardian does not transcribe or interpret recordings automatically.
Not sure what this counts as?
Ask Chat whether something is evidence, diary material, a source link, or just context to keep separate.
Evidence library
Originals, linked records and storage status
EVIDENCE STORAGE
Checking secure storage...
Original files, analysis and confirmed facts are tracked separately. Keep your own untouched backup during testing.
Need to add something?
Use the single Add Evidence screen for files, photos, messages, audio, video and links. This library is for reviewing what is already stored.
Reading document...
Need help organising evidence?
Chat can help you work out what needs a date, what needs the original file, and what should be linked to a Diary entry.
YOUR EVIDENCE CHRONOLOGY
1
Sorted by received/document dateUpload date is kept as audit detail, but the library is organised by when the evidence belongs in the story.
2
Fix weak items quicklyUse filters for missing dates, missing originals and unlinked Diary records.
No evidence yet — use Add Evidence above.
My Case
Update your details anytime
When did contact first begin? Guardian uses this to anchor your diary.
CalmVery difficult
Guardian uses this to keep claims attributed and avoid treating them as proven facts.
Record findings, orders or reports accurately. Guardian will label the source instead of overstating it.
Shapes how Guardian words every reply to avoid language that could be misread as re-engaging.
Helps Guardian keep replies calm, factual and careful with disputed claims.
Paste 10–20 of your sent messages so Guardian sounds like you, not a robot.
Upload exports, documents or screenshots. Guardian learns your natural voice but filters out unsafe habits.
MULTIPLE CASES
Running a C100 and non-mol at the same time? Need to manage a second case? Coming soon.
YOUR DATA AND PRIVACY
Data retention and deletion: Signed-in case data remains in your private account until you delete it. Sensitive local case caches are cleared when you sign out. Technical diagnostics keep only the latest 50 category-and-timing events. Original-file access links expire after 60 seconds.
The export contains your profile, case metadata, analyses, Diary and confirmed facts. Secured originals remain separate and can be downloaded individually from Case File.
TECHNICAL DIAGNOSTICS
Download recent error categories and timings for a tester report. Messages, names and document contents are never included.
Case overview
Your case at a glance
One place to see what matters now
This overview brings together your case profile, Diary, Evidence Library and important dates. Add or correct information in the section it came from.
Understand Your Documents
Take the fear out of every letter
Court documents are written for lawyers, not parents. Upload anything — court order, Cafcass letter, solicitor letter. Guardian reads it and tells you exactly what it means and what to do.
WHAT THIS DOCUMENT IS
WHAT YOU NEED TO DO
⏰ DEADLINE
Prepare for court
Understand documents and prepare an editable draft
Preparation support, not legal advice or court approval
Guardian can organise wording and highlight questions. It cannot decide what is true, predict a result, confirm compliance or file anything. Check every fact, rule and deadline before relying on a draft.
What makes a useful LIP position statement?
Short, hearing-specific, numbered and source-linked. It should make the issues and orders sought easy to find without sounding like copied legal argument. The court order always comes first.
1
Read what came inSeparate claims, dates, requested orders and source mentions before reacting.
2
Check what you will sayKeep your position short, attributed, numbered and focused on this hearing.
3
Export only after reviewGuardian blocks unresolved verification markers and statement-of-truth wording.
Understand a filed document
Organise claims, dates and questions without deciding what is true
Upload a filed document to create a reading guide. Guardian keeps allegations attributed to their author and highlights dates, questions and points to check. It does not decide whether a claim is true or tell you what legal response to make.
A
Directly allegedWhat the document actually says about you, kept attributed to the author.
B
Reported or inferredWhat appears to come from a report, message, witness account or interpretation.
C
Needs checkingDates, documents, missing context and questions to answer before you draft anything.
You do not need to deal with everything at once.
Guardian will separate the document into manageable points. You can ask about any allegation in Chat after it has been read.
Case details for the review guide
These details appear on the printable reading guide and in Chat context. Leave unknown fields blank rather than guessing.
Reality check: if a point is backed by a document, court order, message, witness account or report, Guardian will not minimise it. The aim is to separate evidence, wording, missing context and what you need to check before responding.
WHAT THEY ARE SAYING
ALLEGATIONS OR CLAIMS IDENTIFIED
The labels below describe how much detail appears in this document. They do not predict what the court will decide.
QUESTIONS TO CHECK FACTUALLY
SOURCES OR CLARIFICATION TO LOOK FOR
Safe next steps
1
Do not answer from memory aloneOpen the original source, message, order or diary note before writing a response.
2
Record your factual accountUse the Diary checklist button if you need to verify several points over time.
3
Use Chat for plain EnglishAsk about one point at a time. Guardian will not decide credibility or predict outcome.
Talk it through with Guardian
Chat will carry this reading guide into the conversation. Ask what a section means, what information to check, or how to organise your own factual account.
Add to your case profile?
Guardian found attributed allegations or claims in this document. Add them to your profile only as points to check, not as proven facts.
Ready to respond?
Open an editable drafting workspace using attributed claims and the material you confirm.
Review my draft
Check clarity, attribution and missing support
Upload your draft for a preparation checklist. Guardian can flag unclear wording, unattributed claims, missing dates or sources and language needing review. It cannot grade legal strength or approve a document for filing.
Want to talk through your draft first?
Use Chat if you are unsure what the statement should do, what belongs in it, or why a section feels risky.
PREPARATION CHECKLIST
WHAT NEEDS YOUR REVIEW
WHAT IS WORKING
Talk through this review
Chat can explain the checklist, help you edit unclear parts and practise how to answer questions without over-explaining.
Build an editable starting draft
Guardian organises confirmed material into a draft for you to verify and edit. It is not filing-ready or legal advice.
My Diary
Your memory. Your safety net.
Log what happened
Record family time, an ordinary routine or something positive. The Diary is a balanced record, not only a list of problems.
No Diary entries saved yet.
Unfinished Diary entry
Export diary PDFChoose mode
Use the right export for the job. Court mode removes Guardian coaching and private notes. Personal mode includes completeness coaching. Evidence bundle adds diary, evidence and messages in date order.
Export filters
Personal full export: Includes My Words, structured details, completeness coaching and private Guardian Review. Keep this for your own preparation.

ENTRIES
0
AVG COMPLETENESS
0/10
Start with one same-day entry. A short factual entry is better than waiting for the perfect one.
Planned family timeSet days
Tell Guardian which days are meant to happen. Diary will highlight them and remind you to log what actually happened.
Choose the first date and Guardian selects its weekday automatically. Add the times and repeat pattern, then save it. Add another pattern only if you need one.
Import old diary notes and backup
Paste months of notes from ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, Apple Notes, Google Keep, Word, WhatsApp drafts or any text file. Guardian splits them into dated Diary entries, preserves the original words, labels them imported/reconstructed, then shows what photos, messages, nursery notes or documents would strengthen each entry.
After import: open each weak entry, add the original evidence you still have, then export a cleaner Guardian record.
Backups include diary text, scores and timestamps. Photos should still be kept separately in your phone/cloud storage.
Set a reminder in your phone diary
Until Guardian has full push notifications, set a repeating reminder in your phone calendar so you never forget to log a contact session.
HOW TO SET IT UP
1. Open your phone calendar — Google Calendar, Samsung Calendar, or Apple Calendar
2. Create a new repeating event on your family day — e.g. every Saturday
3. Title it: "Log family time in Guardian"
4. Set the time to the evening — e.g. 7pm after the session
5. Set it to repeat every week on your family day
Even a short entry is valuable. Date, how William was, whether contact happened. That is evidence. That is your record.
Diary tools and settingsExport · schedule · import · backup
Quick Log
Capture the key facts fast
30-second version
Write your truth first. Guardian keeps your original words, then helps you review, strengthen and create a court-safe version only if needed.
Ordinary and positive days matter too. The Diary is not only for problems.
Defaults to today. Tap to change it; your phone will open its normal calendar picker.
More detail: time, source and evidence type
Guardian keeps observations, reports and independent information separate.
Select all that apply — Guardian will calculate your deadlines
Guardian calculates your position statement deadline from this date
Select all that apply
Optional family-time prompts
Use these to pack the diary out quickly. They build the entry summary and suggest useful evidence. You can still ignore them and just write or speak below.
Use this for late return, early return, refused collection, welfare concerns, monitoring messages or anything that needs context.
Private first record. This is for the whole entry, not just concerns. Write naturally: what happened, times if known, who said what, and what evidence exists.
Voice works too — tap the mic and speak naturally. Draft autosaves while you speak.
Add photo previews
These are compressed working previews for the diary. After saving, use Add original evidence to preserve the complete file, filename and SHA-256 integrity hash in Case File.
🔒 Original evidence Available after save

This checks whether useful record fields are present. It does not predict what weight a court would give the entry.

⚖️ Create a court-safe version?
Guardian will convert your entry into factual, court-appropriate language — removing emotion and opinion. Your original entry stays exactly as written.
COURT-SAFE VERSION
Check every fact against My Words and the original evidence. Edit anything Guardian has misunderstood before approving it.
Statement drafting workspace
Editable preparation draft requiring your review
This workspace builds a position statement
It is a concise written position for a particular hearing, not a witness statement and not new evidence. Guardian aims for a human LIP voice with clear numbered paragraphs and traceable sources.
Unsure what route to choose?
Chat can help you decide which statement route fits the hearing without guessing legal strategy.
You may continue without it, but Guardian will mark missing source material for verification.
One sentence is enough. Guardian does the rest.
Document heading
Use the hearing notice or latest order. Guardian will not guess missing court details.
Before drafting: if an important fact is not in Diary, Evidence or your case profile, Guardian will mark it for verification instead of making it up. A polished draft still needs your own checking.
Be specific. Guardian will make sure every section of your statement builds towards this.
Tap a hearing type to see what it means and what your statement needs to achieve.
First hearing — FHDRA
A first hearing in private children proceedings commonly identifies the live issues, considers safeguarding information and decides what agreement, interim arrangements or further directions may be needed. The hearing notice and court directions control what must be prepared.
Your statement should: identify the issues for this hearing, state your position briefly, address relevant safeguarding material with attribution, and set out any practical interim arrangements or directions you want considered.
Contested hearing — dispute resolution
The court may be considering unresolved issues, available reports, possible agreement and whether further directions or a final hearing are required. Check the listing notice for the exact purpose.
Your statement should: confine itself to the unresolved issues, distinguish recommendations from findings, answer only relevant attributed claims, and make a clear practical proposal.
Enforcement hearing — C79
The court is considering an application or issue concerning an existing order. The court, not Guardian, decides whether the order was not complied with and what response is appropriate.
Your statement should: identify the exact order provision, set out specific dated events and sources, preserve the other party's explanation where known, and state precisely what outcome you want considered.
Review hearing
The court ordered a review to check how things are going since the last order or directions. This is your chance to tell the court what has worked, what has not, and what needs to change.
Your statement needs to: report honestly on what has happened since the last hearing, highlight any positives, address any issues, and make proposals for what should happen going forward.
Final hearing
At a final hearing the court may determine the remaining issues after considering the admissible evidence and submissions. A position statement remains a concise guide to your position; it is not a substitute for witness evidence.
Your statement should: identify the remaining issues, accurately cross-reference the evidence, state your child-focused position and end with the exact orders you want the court to consider.

EXPORT YOUR REVIEWED DRAFT
This is an editable preparation draft built from saved material. You own the final wording. Guardian is not a solicitor and this is not legal advice.
Build a clear, source-led statement
Choose a section to see its purpose, what belongs there and what weakens it.
Why Guardian asks for this
A useful position statement is short, dated, attributed and easy to navigate. Guardian uses this guide to organise your draft, not to decide legal strategy or approve it for filing.
Court Bundle
Upload once. Guardian files everything.
Upload your full court bundle and Guardian will read every document inside it, identify each one separately, and file them all to your Case File automatically.
Guardian will identify who filed each document — the court, Cafcass, their solicitor, or you. Best results with digital PDFs from HMCTS. Scanned documents may take longer.
Best results with digital PDFs. Scanned documents may take longer to read.

Backup or scorecard
Tester scorecard
Privacy and limits
Guardian is not a solicitor. It gives organisation, drafting and preparation support. It does not replace legal advice.
Diary entries remain your responsibility. Review every entry before saving. Keep original photos, messages, reports and files separately.
Court exports are factual. Court bundle mode removes Guardian coaching, scores, assumptions and private analysis. Personal exports keep coaching notes for your own preparation.
Patterns are not diagnoses. Guardian may identify repeated communication behaviour, but it must not claim motives, personality disorders or facts that are not evidenced.
Backups matter. Export your diary regularly and store a copy outside Guardian. Before paid users, account backup and recovery must be treated as critical.
Privacy. Tester diagnostics do not include message content, diary text or documents. Account export includes your case records so you can keep your own copy.
Diary entry review
Verified audio transcript
Type or paste the words you can hear. Mark unclear sections as [unclear]. Do not guess. The original recording remains the primary evidence.
